reviews.review_description: Alright I’ll say it as a human we get bored of stuff really quick and that’s how I found myself scrolling through the game store looking for something new to find and play cause I had gotten bored of all my other games that’s how I found Lovesick. I was intrigued by the story but I told myself not to get to attached to another free game that won’t be that good but I was wrong. This game was amazing and it really surprised me I was not expecting it to be this good. And like most people if you keep playing the same game over and over you’re eventually going to get sick of it but not with this game. I’ve played this game so many times and I’m still not bored with it that’s how amazing this is. All I ask is that we get more added to the story that would be so cool if we got to see them again after they had some time to themselves away from the band. That would make an awesome chapter two.

reviews.review_title: I would've have gave it a 5
reviews.review_description: Super fun but one issue when I got to the part with Dom and the merch I could NOT get the board off the ceiling i tried uninstalling and reinstalling still I couldn't get it I tried putting my height at max still couldn't so I recommend it but it might do this to you.

reviews.review_title: the best free game I've ever played
reviews.review_description:
i hope another chapter comes out about them being away from the band and eventually getting back together. I really love this i pray the band get back together. I loved everything about it, there were bugs in it but other than that i loved it sooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo much
make more
I love this game. I am addicted. I would love to be able to join a Discord channel for assistance with some bugs or errors I have been making. This is an amazing game; though, loving it.

When I downloaded this game, I wasn't expecting much, just another free game with probably bad gameplay, but when I started playing, the story, the music, it clicked with me, there have been few games to do that, and I read on another review that this was the last game of the developers, so I say this, it was a blast, perfect for a farewell, in game, and for the developers.

It was one of the best free games I have played so far, but there was a few bugs that made me need to restart from the beginning or a far off checkpoint.

The main one being, occasionally when I would grab an object my hands would get stuck, but grabbing it with the other hand would work unless it was the inventory. In the second to last level my hands got stuck because of the inventory and I had to restart the level. The second glich was just a minor thing, the van keys in the beginning could be grabbed after being used and it just hovers near by. The final glitch is an avoidable glitch with the objects, don't grab and pull with both hands it's funny but not helpful.

Over all this was a good game I do recommend this game.

I love￼sick so much. I appreciate developers work with this game but it isn’t just a game. It’s something else that means a lot more than what some people think it does. It shows you how one team of four  always fights and you bring them through some challenges, that will ￼bring them back together, but they don’t come back together. Maybe you just have to leave them behind but always remember them forever. It’s not all for nothing.￼ It just doesn’t last forever.  Again, I have to appreciate the developers thank you for making this game it really changes my mind on how I think.  🥹￼ moral of the story is appreciate friends as much as you can. Maybe you don’t be friends forever, but it’s not all for nothing. ￼
I loved this game, I’m an idiot and I cannot do puzzles for the life of me but I still enjoyed the freak out of this game

Very short and weird game, you finish it in few minutes. It's about...tanking a bus.. after you're finished, end credits appear. That was so weird that I just quit the game after a minute or so of watching these boring end credits
Amazing game I love the style but it could use some tuning. It’s a bit glitchy and I had to reset the game because of an item I got
Its a great game, I like it because its nostalgic to me, overall this game should be more popular

Finally beat the game, I'd say it's on my top five VR recommendations .It's not perfect and there are some glitches some times but nothing was game breaking and unplayable. Had a blast and maybe asked for hints more times than I should have, this game is definitely worth the time.

This game is unapologetically beautiful.

Every minute detail adds something to the overarching plot. The mechanics are amazing, the puzzles are hard but don't keep you feeling like there's nothing to do, and the locations are dangerously engrossing. This game is genuinely beautiful, and you feel completely immersed in the band as if it were your own. Despite the lack of complex animation, the rich characters dont require it to get through to you. 
Its like they took I Expect You To Die and Scott Pilgrim and made them have a child. The original music for the game is fantastic, and when you finally complete a puzzle, you feel like a genius.

I can't beleive this game is free, it deserves way more than it gets. It is one of the best indie games I've played, and I've played Terraria, Celeste, Pizza Tower, Hollow Knight. This game had better get more recognition and soon.

reviews.review_title: Nice adventure, could use some tuning
reviews.review_description:
If you like adventure games, give LOVESICK a try.

THIS IS THE FINAL GAME from indie studio Rose City Games, which shut down in April 2025. As a result, there will be NO FURTHER UPDATES OR BUG FIXES. I encountered a few bugs in the v1.0.1 patch, though none were game-breaking. YMMV. If something does go wrong, quitting and resuming from an automatic save point might fix it.

Aside from the disappointing rhythm mechanics and the occasional bug, everything else was solid. The story, acting, music, sound design, visuals, animations, and puzzles kept me engaged right up to the ending—which is somewhat predictable, yet (likely unintended) tragically ironic.

I played the game and loved the puzzles and gameplay and rhythm based sections, I encountered a few bugs but still a fun game none the less, my only complaint is you can't change the locomotion and snap turn

What started as a really great adventure came to a sudden stop. 
(Can't grab a board from the ceiling that is crucial for the progress)
Restarting from automatic save point or reinstalling and replaying didn't fix it.
Up to this point i enjoyed the game. Besides the little bugs and glitches  it is a very well executed adventure game.
And as others seem to be able to finish the game you might want to give it a shot.
I’m doms mind I can grab the skateboard the option doesn’t even come up
My game keeps getting stuck at certain parts and I have to fully restart each time I permanently get stuck. Especially at the Motel.
Playing as Sam in a game made me feel like Sam in real life. This game immerses you so well you forgot that you are playing a game until you see the graphics.

I’m not going to lie,I got this for free, whilst i was excited to jump in and discover for myself, some difficult parts just take there off it, the magic hand grabber is hit and miss, I am intrigued by the story and how it all ends, 
Two things took ages to work out, getting the 3D glasses you had to keep hold of the tag remover &
anyone struggling to to get the skateboard down off the beam, take a couple of bottles and throw at the beam to make it collapse & the skateboard falls downs
It’s ok and I’m enjoying it so far.
Teleport is rubbish and would have liked to have free movement.

I want to love this game. I was enjoying it right up until the skateboard up on the rafters. I have tried every trick in the book I can find, but my player just will not interact with that object. Google and all the comments I've read are no help. I've tried all the in game adjustments. Unfortunately I'm going to have to uninstall this game, I give up. I've never had this happen with VR before and I'm disappointed as I was otherwise enjoying the puzzles and pace of the game, and I found the storyline unique and interesting. I hope the devs can get that issue figured out for the rest of you because I am genuinely disappointed I won't complete this game.

For a free to play point and click style game. I genuinely enjoyed it despite some jank from the controls. 
Pros:
-Voice acting is good, background music is nice and not overbearing.

-The art style is charming for a game that I assume was made with limited resources. You could tell they worked in reusing rooms/models into the narrative. I do appreciate that because they didn't really have to draw attention to it. 

-The set pieces are really creative and I love the trippy visuals and swapping genres at the drop of the hat.

-enjoyable puzzles in general

Cons.
-The mechanics are nice but sometimes a bit janky. Mostly the telekinetic like long range grab. The bass controls do take some getting used to. But it seems like the devs are nice enough to not force you to get a perfect score to progress the story. So I'm glad that is not a sticking point.

-required items are not well displayed sometimes. I'll admit I had to check walkthroughs a few times to tell me I could pick this up off the table. Or tell me I can grab a floating object in the void. You get a small circle to indicate you can grab something. But if you aren't zoomed in or on the red travel circle. You sometimes can't tell what's an inventory item.

-some odd choices for checkpoint locations. The most annoying was when I got an arrow to launch at a tree. Of course you can guess you grab the arrow. Then i got the arrow to where it was needed. But after that section I decided to turn the game off. I came back and I thought the arrow was glitched or removed from the game. It was back in the tree and not in my inventory. Stuff like that.

-the story is good imo but the ending is kinda a bummer. Your milage will vary whether you enjoy drama between band members. It was enjoyable to me but I can guess if that sorta thing irks others

Overall a good small time sink. And I feel bad the devs went bankrupt. Which I guess is part of the reasoning for the ending.
Hope you guys do well in the future.
Keep moving forward.

reviews.review_title: This game is so good I just got done
reviews.review_description:
Spoilers, don't look














What the hell they could of worked it out the didn't need to take a two day brake that's it  everything was amazing The gameplay everything like that Just the end they could have made it
The machenits in this game are horrible you need to fix the game
i think it was okay but the one thing that i didn't like about the game is the part where dom the back story as a kid where he had to dodge  tomatoes that just kinda made me frustrated

reviews.review_title: Sad to see no more updates :/
reviews.review_description:
The devs went bankrupt, so no new updates or fixes. I haven’t gotten through the whole game yet, but it has a good story so far. The controls are a bit finicky, but the story (so far) makes up for that.

 The game is a bit too easy to break though; at one point, I had to get 3D glasses and she told me to use the tag remover, problem with that is, I had already used it as I passed it earlier (before she told me to). When I got the glasses, I ￼asked her what to do next, but she just told me to de-tag the 
glasses which I already did; I accidentally sequence broke the game. I had to restart from the beginning. So overall, the game is worth it if you’re one to play adventure games or you just want to spend a boring afternoon. if not, don’t waste your time.

Edit: I just thought, isn’t it ironic that an indie dev studio released game about struggling musicians before they shut down because of their own financial struggles?

reviews.review_title: Looked good but the game is stuck
reviews.review_description: I was enjoying the game as it has a different take, but apparently the “wavelength” option has put the game in stuck mode.  Having done my due diligence and researching the issue I found that many are frustrated with this sensitivity of the mechanics. Unable to move forward with the game as it is “stuck“ until you’re able to utilize this mechanics. It dose not seem that the developers have tweaked this issue until then I am unable to provide an overall rating. Maybe that’s why this only has 3.8 stars.  Love to move forward with this game, but unless the developers patches up this issue, it’s a waste of a few bucks.
Good characters good story I like its stunig art and puzzle mechanics and the music is stunning the only down side is that it had some bugs and that it did not last longer!
It was great but the board is the problem until I grabbed a bottle and used it to drop it so I’m very happy that I played this game and the getting high part was the best

I saw this guy say that all it was was filling a tank and than "end credits" which is not true its called the start credits and someone else said it's a cash grab which is obviously not true just give it a chance

This is the worst VR game ive ever played & ive been playing VR for years, only teleportation movement & click turning, the graphics are shockingly bad, they look as tho as a 5 year kid old worked on them, they all just look like cartoon blobs & they dont move or even their lips dont move when they talk, unless u like wasting money avoid this, its a cash grab attempt from the developers of this so called “VR game”, shockingly bad.
Good game good job mr lovesick

reviews.review_title: Nice story bad graphics and gameplay
reviews.review_description: mouths not being animated unavoidable credits and unclear puzzles were annoying but great game overall
Good game i enjoy it HOWEVER I CANT GET THE SKATEBOARD DOWN TO CONTINUE SO👎
yo guys so i just got done with this game and it's quite sadening the ending and the song is very sad too so

I played this a while ago and it was a pretty good game. Sure there were a few small glitches like my hand flotation away or just not being able to figure out what to do, but it was still a great game with a nice message in the end. Please try it if you can

fix the throwing

make it easier
great puzzle/story game
ROCK  STANISLAUS

This game was not only a blast to play, it also reminds me of lessons i learned years ago. It teaches you that nothing lasts forever, and that not all relationships (romantic or otherwise) are meant to be. This is the highest i can rate this masterpiece, though i wish that i could give it more praise. I highly suggest this game.

Fun to play and has a attaching story
9/10
would be 10 if not for the bugs (there easy to avoid)
It tells a great story and has great music for the punk rock theme, the rhythm aspect worked well and I love the creativity with the powers and using the bass, all around great experience!
This game should not be free, if i knew how good it was i would've played hundreds
Please just play it

This game vibed really well with me. Beyond the janky controls is a little gem of a story game that has great voice acting and was surprisingly longer than I expected. I liked the sound design and music too. I think the first level got more love than the later ones so it was not very consistent but if you like story driven games give LOVESICK a spin!

reviews.review_title: Great but just not for me
reviews.review_description: It was really good I just felt it was a bit slow and not really hard enough with the guitar but still like I said you might like slower games . BUT I will say the graphics are GREAT I mean GREAT

I did love the story of the game, it was a sweet narrative experience, and a good lesson or two with the ending. The style is beautifully cartoonish, characters are relatively well written, and it was generally a fun experience. Voice acting and music was phenomenal.

On the technical side however, there are way too many janky mechanics. Teleporting and blinking can get annoying, the mage hands are generally finicky, and strumming and holding the bass can use a lot of work. Character hints can either give you the answer directly or provide not enough direction.

Despite its flaws, LOVESICK is an amazing game and narrative experience, and I hope future games can shine even brighter. There are many good ideas and a huge amount of potential.
Gameplay can be a bit buggy but regardless, if you’re a millennial, you’ll probably enjoy this quite a bit.

Update: This is my 3rd reinstall, 3rd playthrough and this time the tempo controls are missing and I can't adjust the windmill's speeds...Oh I can adjust the archers speeds but the arrow is missing from the female archer and the cupid statue no longer has it, neither is it in inventory...I'm done! So glad I got it on sale! DEVS why abandon what could be a great game? I don't get it?

Original post: I was really loving this game, it's great! I was halfway through when I upgraded my 3s to a 3 (Which included OS update to). Firstly Lovesick didn't keep my saved position in the game. So I restarted and got to the level where you have to get the drinks to people at the bar only to find the sound waves for grabbing stuff aren't working...at all! Still got the reverb from side trigger. I managed to clear the queue by throwing bottles at them and got to the tomato throwing but nothing is working. The reverb works with nothing in my hand but if I pick up a tomato I can't throw it far enough. Devs!! Help me out, I really want to see the rest of the game but there's lots of bugs!

This is one of the best VR experiences I’ve ever had. I love the story and the art direction fits the grungy aesthetic they are invoking. If you have a quest, get this game.

However, the telekinesis controls can be finicky. I’m deducting a star for just that. Still worth getting

reviews.review_title: Looks like an unfinished alpha version
reviews.review_description: I can live with weird teleport controls from the very first VR games in 2025. And I like the overall game style, but people speaking with closed mouths scare me. But, with all of that devs didn't forget to put unskippable credits intro long as hell at the very beginning, so we could know whom to blame.

reviews.review_title: Good story and narrative bad controls
reviews.review_description: I wanted to love this game and there were parts that were really neat with cool environments but the controls were pretty bad especially trying to play the bass guitar. It's flops around like a flailing arm blowup man outside a car dealership and the grab mechanics are very iffy in parts and your arms get stuck inside walls and other various objects. I gave this 4 stars because I loved the story and the soundtrack was pretty cool. Some of the puzzles were fun and the environments were very cool just the controls are very lacking and can create a very frustrating experience.

Edit: after the 4th reinstall, I had to play entirely from the beginning again and finally was able to progress (although I got zero response from the developers) there was another bug at the very end but after rebooting my headset 4 times I was able to get through. Other than the bugs, and the clunky unfinished controller gameplay, I did enjoy the puzzles, the story, and was pleasantly surprised at the amount of content. I can recommend this on a sale but in it's current state and lack of developer involvement, it's worth about 10 to $12 and you'll most likely feel like you got your money's worth. It does need to be polished and fixed but overall I enjoyed it.

I was really looking forward to this and although I already knew that we were limited to teleport, and the graphics weren't the greatest, and that it was clunky, I wasn't expecting a game breaking bug. This is the second game that has passed through the meta store that has a bug that will keep you from progressing and finishing the game. At least the developers fixed the bug on the other game immediately but I don't even see a response on any of these reviews. I've put time into this and I'm at the graveyard putting together the gravestone and the last piece does not click in it just falls. I have tried uninstalling and reinstalling several times, rebooted my headset, etc and it's still not fixed and of course this is right near the end of the game so I can't get a refund. Again, out of money and they make sure you can't get it back.... If the developers fix this I will update my review but until then I will not recommend this to anybody.

This game is amazing, the story is great and the music and voice acting really add to it.

The controls are odd at times, I personally found times where my hands would get caught on an object and stay there, but for the most part it was a smooth gameplay experience.

The puzzles can be confusing at times and the hints the characters can give vary in quality, but you can definitely figure them all out with some looking around and experimenting.

I had so much fun and I just wish it was longer
Very nice environments. Easy to control/move. Good voiceover. Choices don't matter? I haven't finished the story yet...

LOVESICK exceeded all my expectations for the game. I was first attracted by its distinctive art style, which I truly admire in VR games. Yet, it was the captivating story and gameplay that really kept me engaged, leading me to play straight through the entire three to four hours without a break, as I was having such a great time. The team has crafted a nostalgic point-and-click puzzle game that features a teleport movement system and thoughtfully designed puzzles. While some puzzles may present a challenge, they remain accessible and never veer into the realm of frustration. Even if teleport movement isn’t your preferred style, it’s a deliberate design choice that complements the overall gameplay beautifully, so it’s worth giving it a chance. If you enjoy puzzle games, I highly suggest giving LOVESICK a try.

reviews.review_title: Great premise marred by shaky gameplay
reviews.review_description:
I thought the story, music, and voice acting were top notch. Visually, the game was going for an art deco style and it looks great in the headset (WAY better than the trailers would have you believe). 

Unfortunately the gameplay hurts it. I don't mind teleport & snap turning, but the node based movement was problematic as I would teleport and my hand would get stuck in a wall or shelf or something and be unable to move. The telekinetic grab mechanic was clunky. And the positioning of the guitar felt like it was angled towards the ground and that made those sections difficult to play. 

With some refinement, this game is a winner. The story, characters, and music are all terrific.

I was SUPER looking forward to this as performed in Bands for 15+ years so just having a vr game themed around a band already had me hooked as soon as i heard about it!

Immediately disappointed when teleport AND snap turn was the only way to move.
This wouldnt be an issue if at some locations like the table with the candles i didnt have to lean over to reach them becuase the snap point wasnt as close as it probably it needed to be.

The Bass playing section was just broken, starting off strumming the blue notes up and down as the controller gave the feedback to show it was a thing and seemed ok, as soon as it got to the song playing, strumming up seemed to work 1 every 10 and half of the strumming down completely missed as if the hit box was tiny, i eent to just constantly strumming without stopping and even THEN notes were still missed.

After around 40 mins i will be refunding. 

Gutted.

UPDATE-   I'm done... I was greatly enjoying the game, but now I'm at a section where I need to get the 3D glasses, but my only hint is that I need the "tag remover tool"...
But the ONLY tag removal tool I can find doesn't do anything on thr glasses, but there is NO sign of any tag remover! 3 days I've looked, but very poor design has ruined the experience for me.
Very disappointed, and not sure I can recommend this title anymore..




OG-   The game so far has had good character writing, great music, an excellent look and feel, and ok gameplay that's at least kept my attention from what little I've played.
I REALLY wish they would add Smooth/ Free Rotation though. I can deal with the point & click nature of the game with being teleport only, but Snap Teurn only as well is just being ridiculous...
My one huge issue with the presentation, ESPECIALLY with this being a narratively focused experience more than anything thus far- is that with all of the frequent talking and conversations with other characters, their mouths are NEVER moving. Which really takes me out of it, especially the longer scenes go on where I'm feeling like I'm just seeing dolls being played with while people do voices for them. At one early point, I couldn't even decipher who exactly was speaking in the group between the 2 females because of this mixed with the characters not being very animated in any other wayseither... all of which is sad when everything else was done to such high quality (voice acting, writing, graphics, art direction, music, ect..).

So the lack of mouth animations kills the vibe of this narrative title, but I still say it's absolutely one worth buying.

Just finished the playthrough. I really enjoyed the art style. Bright colors and a unique style that really gives it that punk/rock feel. I thought the voice acting was fantastic. Each character has a unique personality that is discovered as you progress in the story. The puzzles were well thought out. A bit challenging at times but not over the top difficult. Playing the bass takes a little practice. In time I enjoyed it. 

My only little complaint is the characters start getting annoying as you progress more and more. I don't want to spoil anything but let's just say this band likes to point fingers at each other over and over again. 

All in all a really cool experience that I'd recommend.

reviews.review_title: Guitar Hero with Story Mode? Hells yeah!
reviews.review_description:
Adorable mashup of 90s nostalgia, point and click adventure, VR narrative, and.. guitar hero!

I loved my stay with Tommi and the gang - and falling deeper into the weird af rabbit hole the story took me.

There were a few clunky mechanics (particularly the wavelength / force grab), though nothing that distracted me too much from getting through the game.

This is so far my favorite game to come to vr this year. The only reason I bumped it down to a 4 is the couple of bugs that happen through the game. The hand power is a little hard to control and the guitar is a bit frizzy at times but other than that this game was definitely worth a play. All the characters mesh really well and nothing
feels repetitive. So if you like great story telling, puzzles that get you thinking, and baddie goth girls this is the game for you.❤️‍🔥
Very good storytelling and interesting mechanics.
I really love the stylised graphics in VR that age great on VR.
